payload.query: The comparative literature on electoral participation has drawn on two major traditions of research. The first attempts to understand variations in participation on the basis of individual-level data. The second draws on aggregate-level evidence to explore institutional influences on electoral participation. Explain and compare them, focusing on assumptions, hypotheses, and electoral outcomes. Assess the significance of the works you cite. What is the state of our knowledge? Have political scientists been able to establish generalizations?

articles.title: Read the Supreme Court Decision
articles.abstract: The Supreme Court ruled that the race-conscious admissions programs at Harvard and the University of North Carolina were unlawful, curtailing affirmative action at colleges and universities around the nation.
articles.snippet: The Supreme Court ruled that the race-conscious admissions programs at Harvard and the University of North Carolina were unlawful, curtailing affirmative action at colleges and universities around the nation.
